scenarioOrder.label: Task bursts with natural tab order
scenarioOrder.description: Adjacent tabs are often part of the same work burst, with semantic topics spread across domains.

environment.note: Synthetic browser tabs only; no user browsing data or gateway keys are stored.

results.scenarioLabel: Task bursts with natural tab order

settings.customPrompt: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping. Merge related small task fragments during the coarse pass.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

orchestration, and AI coding agent tooling/benchmark references are all part of the same AI agent development effort."},{"bucketKey":"llm_evaluation","title":"LLM Evaluation and Benchmarks","color":"red","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10002,10009,10019,10029,10046,10048],"reason":"Leaderboard comparisons, benchmark papers, and dataset discussions are clearly grouped around LLM evaluation research."},{"bucketKey":"chrome_extension_development","title":"Chrome Extension Development","color":"yellow","confidence":0.94,"tabIds":[10001,10018,10028,10038,10045],"reason":"Side panel lifecycle and Chrome extension API permission/tabGroups references are all implementation resources for browser extensions."},{"bucketKey":"frontend_implementation_testing","title":"Frontend Implementation & Testing","color":"green","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10004,10014,10021,10031,10041],"reason":"CSS implementation, frontend state debugging, and Playwright smoke test fixture work all belong to engineering frontend execution tasks."},{"bucketKey":"cloudflare_infra","title":"Cloudflare Gateway Work","color":"purple","confidence":0.93,"tabIds":[10003,10013,10030,10040],"reason":"Worker routing/secrets and Cloudflare tunnel diagnostics tabs are all Cloudflare infrastructure/worker ops references."},{"bucketKey":"database_sql","title":"Database and SQL Engineering","color":"cyan","confidence":0.91,"tabIds":[10005,10022,10024,10032],"reason":"Migration checklists, SQL notes, and query plan optimization are all database engineering content."},{"bucketKey":"product_ui_design","title":"Product UI Design","color":"pink","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10010,10012,10020,10039,10047,10049],"reason":"Side panel/compact UI patterns, README layout references, and switch pattern examples are design-focused product UI research."},{"bucketKey":"media_learning","title":"Video and Media Queue","color":"grey","confidence":0.89,"tabIds":[10006,10016,10026,10033,10043],"reason":"All selected items are tutorial/talk video references for media-consumption during the work session."},{"bucketKey":"shopping_finance","title":"Shopping and Finance","color":"red","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10007,10017,10034,10036,10044],"reason":"Checkout, pricing, billing, and finance pages are part of personal or procurement/financial comparison and payments context."},{"bucketKey":"read_later","title":"Read Later","color":"blue","confidence":0.88,"tabIds":[10015,10023,10025,10042],"reason":"Long-form essays, references, and newsletter pages are explicitly read-later style background materials."},{"bucketKey":"security_review","title":"Security Notes","color":"yellow","confidence":0.85,"tabIds":[10011],"reason":"Security checklist is a single focused safety/compliance resource separate from other implementation threads."}],"reviewTabIds":[]}

messages.content:
You are a JSON-only cleanup ranking planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Shape: {"summary":"Short summary","candidates":[{"id":1,"priority":"high|medium|low","reason":"Why review this tab","evidence":["signal"]}]}
This is a manual review checklist, not an automatic close command.
Use high for likely stale/duplicate/superseded/finished tabs, medium for plausible cleanup items, and low for low-urgency items that still help the user review the tab set.
Rank as many eligible tabs as useful up to cleanupInstructions.actionLimit. For sessions at or below that limit, include nearly all tabs unless they are clearly current, pinned, or unsafe to suggest.
Keep each candidate compact: reason should be one short clause, evidence should contain one or two short signals.
Use original tab order, tab age/activity, current groups, page summaries, and the proposed grouping context.
Do not recommend closing pinned tabs as high priority unless evidence is very strong.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
